methods.description: Evaluation of climate-tree-ring-growth relationships using standard correlation function (CF) and response function (RF) analysis to understand the impact of climate on tree growth.

steps.description: Climate-tree-ring-growth relationships of the four chronologies were evaluated by means of the standard correlation function (CF) and response function (RF) analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform correlation and response function analysis on tree-ring data

methods.method_name: Standardization and Comparison of Time Series
methods.description: Standardization of tree-ring indices and temperature values to facilitate comparison by computing z-scores, resulting in time series with zero mean and unit variance.

steps.description: Tree-ring standardized residual indices and June-July (JJ) mean temperature values were scaled computing z-scores by subtraction of the mean and division by the standard deviation over their entire length.
steps.searchable_summary: compute z-scores for tree-ring indices and temperature values

methods.description: Analysis of the stability of mean sensitivity over time to delineate the response of tree-ring dataset to climate.

steps.description: Checked the stability of mean sensitivity over time.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stability of mean sensitivity in tree-ring data

methods.method_name: Tree-Ring Data Collection and Chronology Development
methods.description: Collection of tree-ring data and development of chronologies using standard dendrochronological methods, including sampling, measuring, cross-dating, and detrending.

steps.description: Sampling procedures were conducted below the treeline selecting dominant and undisturbed trees without stem or crown anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: select and sample undisturbed trees for tree-ring data

steps.description: Tree-ring widths were measured to the nearest 0.01 mm by means of a LINTAB increment measuring table.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree-ring widths using LINTAB table

steps.description: Cross-dated the samples visually and statistically with the TSAPwin software.
Calculated residual chronologies using ARSTANwin by applying a double detrending method.
Checked the correct cross-dating using the program COFECHA.
Assessed the adequacy of the replication in the early years of the four chronologies using the subsample signal strength (SSS).
